nodes.title: Mosaic: A Visual Analogy JavaScript Learning Tool
Incongruity Theory in the Philosophy of Humor
nodes.description: In this paper, I will first explain Platonism and its relation to mathematics and reconstruct arguments against it to show how mathematical objects ultimately cannot exist. I will then explore logicism and formalism in order to critically evaluate how they create truth for mathematical propositions and the problems that they have that could or should prevent mainstream philosophical adoption. Finally, I will explain Benacerraf’s structuralism and why I think it is the best Anti-Platonist explanation for the philosophical foundation of mathematics.

nodes.title: Advancing Epistemological Pluralism Between Text and Block-Based Coding
Evaluating Anti-Platonist Approaches to Mathematics
nodes.description: In this paper, I will first explain Platonism and its relation to mathematics and reconstruct arguments against it to show how mathematical objects ultimately cannot exist. I will then explore logicism and formalism in order to critically evaluate how they create truth for mathematical propositions and the problems that they have that could or should prevent mainstream philosophical adoption. Finally, I will explain Benacerraf’s structuralism and why I think it is the best Anti-Platonist explanation for the philosophical foundation of mathematics.

nodes.title: Uprooting the Flame
nodes.description: In this paper, I will argue that, following the advice of the Buddhists and Stoics, we should extirpate anger from ourselves for the betterment of ourselves, and to a greater extent, our society.

nodes.title: Lying: Moral Choice in Public and Private Life by Sissela Bok
nodes.description: Is it ever all right to lie? A philosopher looks at lying and deception in public and private life—in government, medicine, law, academia, journalism, in the family and between friends.

nodes.title: Theoretical Virtues of Major Theories of Time
nodes.description: In this paper, I have two main goals. The first of which is to develop an account of why people believe or choose to believe certain scientific and/or metaphysical theories, with an focus on theories of time. In particular, I will argue that people choose to believe intuition-preserving theories of time (intuition-preservation as a theoretical virtue). Additionally, I consider if this is a good thing or not, and whether people should evaluate theories on the basis of intuition-preservation. The second goal is to use the developed account to examine the theoretical virtues of some of the most popular competing metaphysical theories of time, namely: Presentism, Growing Block Theory, and Eternalism.

nodes.title: The Moral Judgement of the Child by Jean Piaget
nodes.description: The Moral Judgement of the Child traces children's moral thinking from preschool to adolescence, tracing their concepts of lying, cheating, adult authority, punishment, and responsibility and offering important insights into how they learn -- or fail to learn -- the difference between right and wrong.

nodes.title: I Want to Die But I Want to Eat Tteokboki by Baek Seehee
nodes.description: Recording her dialogues with her psychiatrist over a twelve-week period, and expanding on each session with her own reflective micro-essays, Baek begins to disentangle the feedback loops, knee-jerk reactions, and harmful behaviors that keep her locked in a cycle of self-abuse. Part memoir, part self-help book, I Want to Die but I Want to Eat Tteokbokki is a book to keep close and to reach for in times of darkness. It will appeal to anyone who has ever felt alone or unjustified in their everyday despair.

nodes.description: The concept of romance doesn't always serve everyone. Can we rehabilitate this concept or are we better off abandoning it entirely?

nodes.title: Everything is not romantic
nodes.description: My proposal for getting away from using the word "romance" to avoid conceptual baggage and adding to the weight of the term.

nodes.title: Romance Is Prescriptive
nodes.description: Romance has prescriptive power which can limit your relationships.

nodes.title: Ghibli-fication and AI "Art"
nodes.description: A lot of so-called "AI Art" might be style transfer, which I argue is not art at all.

nodes.title: Being (W)Asian
nodes.description: A reflection on personal identity and of growing up and being White and Asian.

nodes.title: Conspiracy by Contrapoints

nodes.title: Mechanisms of Socialization

nodes.description: My notes, analysis, and extension of thought on the wonderful article about Hermeneutic Labor by Ellie Anderson.

nodes.title: Attached by Amir Levine and Rachel S. F. Heller
nodes.description: Based on the science of attachment, the book looks at the three basic types of attachment: avoidant, anxious, and secure.

nodes.title: Finding Lifting Exercises That Are Right For Me
nodes.description: Exercise routines only need be right for you and not anyone else.

nodes.title: Polyamory Acts on More Than Just Romance
nodes.description: I think that the greatest benefit of non-monogamy is not just increased romance in your life, but increased potential for closeness with all types of relationships.

nodes.title: My Interior Design Philosophy

nodes.title: Re-imagining vs De-centering Romance
nodes.description: Can you re-imagine romance without de-centering it or vice versa?

nodes.title: Why Do People Stay In Bad Relationships?
nodes.description: Exploring the microeconomics/behavioral economics of staying in a bad relationship.

nodes.title: Asexuality and Sexual Desire/Pleasure
nodes.description: In this article, I argue for a more pleasure-centric understanding of asexuality as a way to make introspection and identification easier.

nodes.title: Is non-monogamous love shallower?
nodes.description: I think that this is a question that monogamous and non-monogamous people have all thought about at least once, so I wanted to dedicate some time thinking about it to settle the question.

nodes.title: On Training Men To Be Boyfriends
nodes.description: An attempt to explain and ameloriate the experience of "training" your boyfriend.

nodes.title: Vegan, Nut-Free, High-Protein Packaged Snacks
nodes.description: A list of snacks in order of best-to-worst CTP ratio.

nodes.title: Anabolic Plant-Based Protein

nodes.title: Are shared goals required for a partnership?
nodes.description: My take on why shared goals aren't required for a partnership, but that shared values and intentions are more important.

nodes.title: On Living Together
nodes.description: Thinking critically about the idea of partners living together and what alternatives there may be.

nodes.title: Mutual Support in Friendship
nodes.description: Discussing a conception of mutual support in friendship as an analog to mutual aid in community.

nodes.title: One Love
nodes.description: My argument for there not being any different types of love.

nodes.description: Exploring a model separate from partnership to ascribing importance to certain relationships in our lives.

nodes.title: What does growing together in a relationship mean?
nodes.description: Comparing three possible senses of the phrase where none of them are "incorrect", but will depend on a per-relationship basis.

nodes.title: What is play?
nodes.description: An exploration into what play is (and isn't).

nodes.title: Philosophical Concepts Mentioned in R.F. Kuang's Katabasis
nodes.description: A list of philosophical concepts in R.F. Kuang's Katabasis, augmented by my recommended further readings.

nodes.title: Dopamine Menu
nodes.description: A list of various things that can give a person dopamine curated from other resources that I've found online.

nodes.title: Quarterlife: The Search for Self in Early Adulthood by Satya Doyle Byock
nodes.description: Quarterlife is a defining work that offers a compassionate roadmap toward finding understanding, happiness, and wholeness in adulthood.

nodes.title: Texting should be more like email
nodes.description: Drawing on features that make email a better model of conversation and how they could be integrated to texting.

nodes.title: Don't Break Links
nodes.description: Why you shouldn't break links on your personal site and how to do redirects in Jekyll.

nodes.title: The Pile Method of Cleaning
nodes.description: A detailed look on how to execute my personal way of cleaning.

nodes.title: Teasing, Play, and Flirtation

nodes.title: How much more expensive is eating plant-based?

nodes.description: Analyzing and comparing my lifting progress for three of my lifts: Leg Extension, Preacher Curl, and Lateral Raise.

nodes.title: Materialists (2025): Review

nodes.title: A Pigeon Sat On A Branch Reflecting On Existence (2014): Review
nodes.description: An absurdist, surrealistic and shocking pitch-black comedy, which moves freely from nightmare to fantasy to hilariously deadpan humour as it muses on man’s perpetual inhumanity to man.

nodes.title: Comedic Sparring
nodes.description: A look into a phenomenon that I don't think is discussed much or given any in-depth analysis of a certain kind of verbal sparring including humor.

nodes.title: Favorite Directors
nodes.description: A list of my favorite film directors.

nodes.title: Thoughts on Polynormativity
nodes.description: Drawing together my thoughts on polynormativity through a few different articles; some that I've written, some that I have not.

nodes.title: By The Stream (2024): Review

nodes.title: Riffing is Human Nature
nodes.description: A look into an underappreciated pastime: What is riffing? How do you do it? Why do we do it?

nodes.title: Warfare (2025): Review
nodes.description: A platoon of American Navy SEALs on a surveillance mission gone wrong in insurgent territory. A boots-on-the-ground story of modern warfare and brotherhood, told in real time and based on the memory of the people who lived it.

nodes.description: A medieval knight plays a game of chess with the personification of Death who has come to take his life.

nodes.title: Caught By The Tides (2025): Review
nodes.description: Years after her boyfriend left her for the big city and promised to bring her there after he’s settled down, a Chinese woman sets out on a journey to be reunited with him.

nodes.title: Marjorie Prime (2017): Review
nodes.description: A service which creates holographic projections of late family members allows an elderly woman to spend time with a younger version of her deceased husband.

nodes.title: Yi Yi (2000): Review
nodes.description: Each member of a Taipei family asks hard questions about life’s meaning as they experience everyday quandaries. NJ is morose: his brother owes him money, his mother-in-law is in a coma, his wife suffers a spiritual crisis when she finds her life a blank and his business partners make bad decisions.

nodes.title: A Year of Home Updates 2025

nodes.title: Evil Does Not Exist (2023): Review

nodes.description: A reflection of what I wrote about and what that said about what was on my mind this year.

nodes.description: Love and care goes both ways: an exploration into "being hard to love".

nodes.description: A look at how I worked over two years to improve content search and discovery interactivity in my digital garden on this site.

nodes.title: About Endlessness (2019): Review
nodes.description: A reflection on human life in all its beauty and cruelty, its splendor and banality, guided by a Scheherazade-esque narrator. Inconsequential moments have the same significance as historical events. Simultaneously an ode and a lament, presents a kaleidoscope of all that is eternally human, an infinite story of the vulnerability of existence.

nodes.title: The Moral Philosophy of The Drama (2026)
